title: Rotting Oranges
prompt:
Given a 2D grid where:
- 0 represents an empty cell,
- 1 represents a fresh orange,
- 2 represents a rotten orange,
return the minimum number of minutes until all oranges are rotten.

Each minute, any fresh orange adjacent (up, right, down, left) to a rotten orange becomes rotten.
If it is impossible to rot all oranges, return -1.
If there are no oranges, return 0.
constraintQuestions: What if no fresh oranges exist? (Return 0.)
What if fresh oranges can never be reached? (Return -1.)
Can there be multiple starting rotten oranges? (Yes.)

walkthrough.title: Multi-source BFS
walkthrough.body:
Scan the grid to collect all rotten oranges into a queue and count fresh oranges.
Perform BFS level by level (each level is a minute).
When a fresh orange is reached, rot it and decrement the fresh count.
At the end, if fresh > 0 return -1; otherwise return minutes.

codeSnippets.title: JavaScript
C#

walkthrough.title: Complexity
walkthrough.body:
Time: O(r*c) because each cell is processed at most once.
Space: O(r*c) for the queue in the worst case.
